qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: AgentOS continue-run deserialization evaluates client-supplied field types and enables server-side code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fix commit replaces the same attacker-controlled `field_type` `eval()` sink in `UserInputField.from_dict()` that this finding reaches through continue-run deserialization, so it identifies the advisory's vulnerable code/root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Knowledge URL uploads trigger unrestricted server-side requests to attacker-chosen destinations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is an SSRF in the knowledge upload router and does not involve the advisory's attacker-controlled `field_type` evaluation path or the fixed `eval()` sink.

selected_finding.title: AgentOS continue-run deserialization evaluates client-supplied field types and enables server-side code execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding reaches the same attacker-controlled `field_type` `eval()` sink fixed by GHSA-77rh-m34w-rv36, and the copied PoC demonstrated attacker-controlled Python execution in the AgentOS process through that sink.
evidence: GHSA-77rh-m34w-rv36 describes attacker-controlled `field_type` reaching `eval()` in Agno before 2.3.24, and fix commit `cbf675521d4d2281925a051784a3b94172e56416` replaces that `eval()` in `libs/agno/agno/tools/function.py` and related model parsing logic.

At vulnerable commit `d667f085b3392f9c66719caca364ef3cc73a3f7b`, the continue-run route deserializes attacker-supplied `tools` into `ToolExecution.from_dict()`, which rebuilds `user_input_schema` fields through `UserInputField.from_dict()` and reaches `eval(data["field_type"])`.

The copied PoC run log shows a benign continuation returned status 200 without creating the proof file, then a malicious continuation returned status 200 and printed `EVOHUNT_PROOF: continue-run unsafe eval created the proof file` after creating `/tmp/evohunt-continue-run-eval-proof.txt`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC completed successfully; a benign continue-run request left the proof path absent, then a malicious `field_type` continuation payload returned 200 and created `/tmp/evohunt-continue-run-eval-proof.txt`, and the log printed `EVOHUNT_PROOF: continue-run unsafe eval created the proof file`.
